coverage.boundary: Named public waters and officially documented public access facilities are included. Private, user-supplied secret, unauthorized, or conservation-sensitive locations are excluded.

currentNotices: Zone-fishing closures exist near floating bridges.
Waterbody- and species-specific rules must be checked before fishing.
Fish-consumption advisories apply to specified species and sizes.
directVerification: Check Fish Washington for current emergency rules.
Confirm the selected launch or pier is open on the intended date.

currentNotices: Selective gear rules apply.
Catch and release applies to all species.
Bait and internal-combustion motors are prohibited.
Shore fishing is limited to the boat-launch area.
directVerification: Check emergency rule changes before departure.
Confirm access-site conditions and parking availability.

publicAccess.name: Boyd Lake State Park main entrance
Main boat ramp by the marina

currentNotices: Boating is closed for the remainder of 2026 because of low water levels.
Hand-launched kayaks and paddleboards remain allowed.
The marina is closed for the 2026 season.
Fire restrictions require same-day confirmation.
directVerification: Check CPW closures and conditions on the day of travel.
Confirm current fire restrictions.
Check current fishing regulations and water-level conditions.

currentNotices: Lower water can expose unmarked hazards.
Boat capacity limits may temporarily stop launches.
Motorized or trailered vessels require ANS inspection, stamp, and current registration.
New Zealand mudsnails are present.
directVerification: Confirm ramp and inspection hours.
Check current reservoir level, weather, closures, and fishing regulations.

destinations.accessSite: West Shore Unit, Flathead Lake State Park
currentNotices: Dock availability is seasonal and weather dependent.
A Conservation License or applicable access requirement may apply.
Aquatic invasive species inspection rules apply to watercraft entering the Flathead Basin.
directVerification: Confirm dock, campground, and launch status.
Check current Montana fishing regulations and Flathead Basin AIS requirements.

destinations.accessSite: Finley Point Unit, Flathead Lake State Park

publicAccess.name: Finley Point ADA-accessible reservable boat slip

currentNotices: The park is within the Flathead Reservation.
A tribal fishing license is required for fishing on the southern half of Flathead Lake.
Recreation on tribal land outside the park is subject to tribal permits and rules.
directVerification: Confirm the exact jurisdiction and license required for the intended fishing area.
Check current park, tribal, fishing, weather, and AIS notices.

publicAccess.name: Granite SWA Arkansas River fishing area

currentNotices: Access is permitted only while fishing.
Launching or taking out vessels is prohibited.
Dogs, mining, and panning are prohibited.
A valid fishing license or SWA pass is required for most visitors age 16 or older.
directVerification: Check current SWA restrictions and closures.
Check Arkansas River flows, weather, and fishing regulations before travel.

publicAccess.name: Montana FWP and BLM Madison River access sites

currentNotices: Upper and lower river segments have materially different conditions and use patterns.
Commercial, competitive, and organized group use requires a Special Recreation Permit.
Public stream use does not authorize crossing posted private land.
directVerification: Select the intended river segment and official access site.
Check current fishing restrictions, flows, closures, fire conditions, and access-site status.

publicAccess.name: Cabin Creek property via U.S. Forest Service Road 41

currentNotices: Parking and access are limited.
A Discover Pass or qualifying Vehicle Access Pass is required on WDFW lands.
Wildfire restrictions may limit activities and remain subject to change.
directVerification: Confirm road, parking, wildfire, and property access status.
Check current fishing regulations for the intended stream segment.

destinations.waterbody: Florida tidal shoreline and public saltwater piers
speciesContext: Marine and estuarine species vary by coast, season, tide, salinity, and local regulation
currentNotices: Saltwater license and permit requirements vary by residency, fishing method, species, and whether a pier or vessel license covers anglers.
Tides, weather, salinity, closures, and species rules require destination-specific confirmation.
Shore-based shark fishing requires an additional permit for anglers age 16 and older.
directVerification: Choose a specific official pier, bridge, jetty, ramp, or shoreline destination before travel.
Check current FWC saltwater regulations, licenses, permits, tides, weather, and local closures.
